title: derenbergia halenbergensis
side_effects: Derenbergia halenbergensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation and allergic reactions, especially when handling the plant's sap or leaves. Ingesting any part of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's pollen can trigger respiratory issues, including asthma attacks and congestion. In rare cases, improper use of Derenbergia halenbergensis can also cause anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction.
